name: Razorpay API Gateway — Rate Limiting & Key Auth
scenario: Razorpay's payment API is being abused by a compromised merchant key sending 5000 req/min, crashing the service for legitimate merchants. Implement per-merchant rate limiting with Bucket4j, API key authentication, and idempotency keys.

core.description: ApiKeyAuthFilter: validates X-API-Key header against a store (Map<String,String> key→merchantId). 401 if missing, 403 if invalid. Sets 'merchantId' request attribute on success.
RateLimiterService: Bucket4j LoadingCache<String,Bucket>, 100 req/min per merchant (classic bandwidth, greedy refill). isAllowed(merchantId) → boolean.

RateLimitFilter: OncePerRequestFilter — reads merchantId from request attribute, checks rate limiter. Allowed: set X-RateLimit-Remaining header + chain.doFilter. Denied: 429 with Retry-After:60 and JSON body.
IdempotencyService: Cache<String,String> (24h TTL). checkIdempotency(key) → Optional<String>. markProcessed(key, paymentId).
Micrometer counter: rate.limit.exceeded with merchant tag. Incremented on every blocked request.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): valid key+allowed→200, invalid key→403, rate exceeded (drain bucket)→429, idempotency dedup→same paymentId returned. All 4 pass.
README: explains per-merchant vs global rate limiting, why Redis-backed is needed for distributed.

bonus.description: Tiered rate limits: STANDARD=100, PREMIUM=500, ENTERPRISE=2000 req/min. merchantId→plan resolved from API key store. Filter selects correct bucket per plan.
PromQL alert rules YAML file: RateLimitAbuseDetected (>50 exceeded in 5min), ApiKeyAbuse (>100 invalid attempts in 1min). Both with annotations and runbook_url.
MetricRateLimiter: Gauge for current token level per merchant. @SpringBootTest: gauge reads correct value after consuming tokens.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Bucket4j LoadingCache: Caffeine.newBuilder().expireAfterAccess(1,HOURS).build(key -> newBucket()) — creates bucket lazily per merchantId. tryConsume(1): returns true if token available (allow request) or false (deny, return 429). X-Forwarded-For: use this header for real IP behind load balancer (split on comma, take first). OncePerRequestFilter: guaranteed single execution per request — no duplicate rate limit checks. Per-merchant not global: critical design choice — each merchant has isolated bucket (one attacker's 429 doesn't affect others).
